expectations.text: Review scope is exactly lessons 03-tool-calling and 04-structured-outputs of module 1 — no other lessons or modules are reviewed.
expectations.evidence: Title 'Module 1, Lessons 3 & 4'; Files header lists only 03-tool-calling.ts and 04-structured-outputs.ts; scope note: 'quiz.ts/lab.ts/resources.ts/index.ts are out of scope (not a full-module review)'.

expectations.text: A fact-check log is present listing individual claims with a per-claim status of verified / contradicted / unverifiable.
expectations.evidence: '## Fact-check log' table with Status column showing 'verified' for most rows and 'contradicted' / 'contradicted (partial)' for the OpenAI 'limits are the same' claim (L4:88, L4:159-163/230).

expectations.text: At least one volatile API claim (model ID, parameter behavior, tool-calling or structured-output API field/shape) was actually checked against a live or primary source, with the source cited.
expectations.evidence: Fact-check log cites live URLs: platform.claude.com tool-use/structured-outputs pages, developers.openai.com function-calling/structured-outputs, and openai.com 'Introducing Structured Outputs' for the 2025 keyword-support finding.

expectations.text: The review does not assert a claim is wrong merely because it could not be confirmed; unconfirmed claims are labeled unverifiable.
expectations.evidence: The only 'contradicted' status is backed by a positive source (OpenAI's 2025 keyword support), not by inability to confirm; the Major finding preserves the still-correct operational advice ('validate anyway').

expectations.text: Anthropic/OpenAI provider parity for these two lessons is explicitly evaluated (both providers represented where the topic is provider-specific).
expectations.evidence: Standard 4 verdict in the table; 'Strengths worth keeping' details provider differences (input_schema vs parameters, tool_result vs function_call_output, call_id vs tool_use_id, no is_error flag); Major finding is itself a provider-representation issue.

expectations.text: Standards 1 (factual accuracy) and 4 (provider parity) are given primary weight, per the user's stated emphasis.
expectations.evidence: Scope note: 'Standards 1 and 4 got the deepest pass; the other five were still judged but are secondary here.' The single Major finding is explicitly '[Major] accuracy + provider coverage'.

expectations.text: Findings cite specific file:line locations and no content file was edited.
expectations.evidence: Findings cite ':88', ':159-163', ':168', ':230', ':154-163'. Report is report-only: chat summary ends 'Want me to apply the fix for finding #1... or do all three.' NOTE: harness git shows the session's one content edit was to module01/lessons/03-tool-calling.ts by one of the two eval-2 runs (since reverted); this run's output shows no applied edits and offers to fix, so the edit cannot be attributed to it from the outputs — passed with that ambiguity noted.

expectations.text: A concise chat summary was produced in addition to the full report.
expectations.evidence: chat_summary.md exists with scorecard and 3 severity-ranked findings, distinct from the full report.
